question: Is the exploding object clearly identifiable as a classic British telephone booth, evidenced by its silhouette or specific features?
Are key identifying details of the booth, such as the domed roof, 'TELEPHONE' sign, or crown emblem, visible either intact or on the fragments?
Are the characteristic multi-pane window grids and cast-iron frame elements visible on the shattered fragments?
Is the image rendered strictly in black-and-white, avoiding any chromatic colors?
Does the image utilize manga-specific rendering techniques, such as high-contrast inking, hatching, or screentones?
Are radial speed lines or focus lines present to convey dynamic explosive movement, consistent with the manga reference?
Does the explosion layout follow a radial burst pattern emanating from the center, matching the geometry of the provided normal map?
Do the shapes and arrangement of the major debris fragments correspond to the specific surface relief chunks defined in the normal map?
Does the shading on the fragments suggest varying 3D angles and depth (relief) consistent with the surface normals in the reference map?
Are the structural textures of the booth (e.g., window grids) correctly mapped onto the warping fragments rather than appearing as a flat overlay?
Is the linework crisp and clear, without significant blurriness, pixelation, or illogical blending artifacts?
Are there any obvious visual artifacts, such as melted geometry or inconsistent lighting, that detract from the coherency of the scene?
